headline: Dual Language Programme Covers Only 2,100 of 7,800 Schools
context: The DLP allowing English-medium science and math teaching has expanded slowly since its 2016 launch.

cards.big: Only 2,100 schools enrolled in the Dual Language Programme 10 years after launch
cards.sub: Participation is voluntary leaving 73% of schools outside the program

cards.big: DLP students score 14% higher on English proficiency tests than non-DLP peers
cards.sub: Language exposure through STEM subjects creates measurable competency gains

cards.big: 58% of eligible schools cited lack of English-proficient teachers as barrier to DLP
cards.sub: Teacher language competency is the primary constraint on expansion

cards.big: European schools using CLIL methodology report 30% higher bilingual achievement rates
cards.sub: Content and Language Integrated Learning provides a proven pedagogical framework

cards.big: Voluntary bilingual education creates a two-tier system where geography determines opportunity
Should the DLP be made mandatory for all schools with adequate English teacher capacity
